title: The Fan of The Death
wikitext:
[]: The Fan of The Death             Quote Post by Nightquest » Mon Feb 14, 2011 9:05 pm I noticed this some time ago, but I couldn't find any kind of reference related to this, that's why I'm asking here. I'm talking about the fan which appears behind Kaji at the time of his death: In "Death Note", we can see it a few minutes before Kira's death: And then, in Silent Hill 2 (the videogame): I can't remember exactly when it takes place, but we know "death" is sort of the main subject in the wholoe story. In the two previous cases (Kaji and Kira) they were both waiting for their death. What do you think? Is it a reference to an old move or something like that or Anno just did it because it looks nice?

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Feb 14, 2011 10:06 pm I always thought the Death Note example was a reference to Eva. Never noticed the Silent Hill one, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Nightquest » Mon Feb 14, 2011 10:22 pm TehDonutKing wrote: I always thought the Death Note example was a reference to Eva. Never noticed the Silent Hill one, though.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Feb 14, 2011 10:48 pm Probably.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Feb 14, 2011 10:55 pm I know in the case of Silent Hill, it was done simply because it was "unnerving" and "invoked thoughts of cycles."

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Feb 14, 2011 11:26 pm This thread makes me want to sing about the future in front of a giant fan.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Tue Feb 15, 2011 1:50 am It's an additional horror element added solely for the Korean audiences.

[]:             Quote Post by Combs » Tue Feb 15, 2011 1:51 am The fan = four blades = four = shi = death

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Feb 15, 2011 1:54 am Dr. Nick wrote: It's an additional horror element added solely for the Korean audiences.

[]:             Quote Post by Nightquest » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:08 am Dr. Nick wrote: It's an additional horror element added solely for the Korean audiences.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:36 am If we want to search for this kind of imagery, there's a scene in Total Recall (yes, the Arnie film) where the baddie cuts of air supply to some sector, threatening its inhabitants with death. For dramatic effect, it's shown as the ductwork system's fans are slowing down.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:44 am Nightquest wrote: Thanks! That was the answer I was looking for :) I'd never imagine that it was called "fan death". I can't believe people thought a fan could kill you because of a vortex which sucks the oxygen :/

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:51 am But, but, but the fan behind Kaji clearly has more than four blades.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:52 am BornIn1142 wrote: But, but, but the fan behind Kaji clearly has more than four blades.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Feb 15, 2011 7:20 am In the OP of Noir, Mireille, who is played by Misato's seiyuu, Kotono Mitsuishi, appears with the Fan of Death: Was this intended as an in-joke reference to the popular, but erroneous, fan theory that Misato shot Kaji?

[]:             Quote Post by Nightquest » Tue Feb 15, 2011 10:36 am Xard wrote: I'm pretty sure Dr. Nick wasn't being serious

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Feb 15, 2011 10:43 am yeah, looks like my hikky depression is starting to get best of me. Guess I should retire from internet for a while to sharpen my senses again ...oh right that's not a real option because I have nothing else to do

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Feb 15, 2011 1:40 pm *reads that wikipedia link.* Holy SHIT, Koreans are stupid.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Tue Feb 15, 2011 4:59 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Holy SHIT, Koreans are stupid.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Feb 15, 2011 5:28 pm I used to do that all the time.
